# Phase 4A — Per-Tenant Ingestion Observability Daemon (KBRecorderService Extension)

## Context

Sub of Phase 4 Epic [#11628](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/issues/11628) (meta-Epic [#11624](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/issues/11624)).

**Extends existing KBRecorderService for multi-tenant telemetry.** No new database; reuses Memory Core SQLite substrate per `KBRecorderService` precedent.

## The Problem

Once Phase 2 push pipeline ships, cloud operators have no visibility into per-tenant ingestion health: push frequency, error rates, ingestion latency, embedding-budget burn, schema-version drift, etc. Without observability, operators can't:

- Detect tenant abuse (excessive pushes, embedding budget exhaustion)
- Detect tenant errors (silent push failures, schema mismatches)
- Plan capacity (per-tenant chunk growth rates)
- Surface health in operator dashboards

## The Fix

Extend [`KBRecorderService.mjs`](../tree/dev/ai/services/knowledge-base/KBRecorderService.mjs) (currently captures KB QUERY telemetry → projects to `kb_query_faqs`) to also capture INGESTION telemetry:

1. **New SQLite table `kb_ingestion_events`** in shared Memory Core database:
   - `tenantId`, `agentIdentity`, `eventType` (push/parse/embed/error), `timestamp`, `chunkCount`, `durationMs`, `bytesIngested`, `errorCode?`, `schemaVersion`
2. **`KnowledgeBaseIngestionService.ingestSourceFiles` (Phase 2A) emits telemetry events** to `KBRecorderService.recordIngestionEvent({...})`
3. **Aggregation projection** (similar to `kb_query_faqs`): per-tenant rolling-window metrics materialized into `kb_tenant_ingestion_health` table
   - Per-tenant push frequency (events/hour)
   - Per-tenant error rate (errors/total)
   - Per-tenant chunk growth rate
   - Per-tenant embedding-budget burn (chunks × embedding cost)
4. **Daemon process**: `ai/scripts/kb-observability-daemon.mjs` (sibling to existing daemons) runs aggregation projection periodically + writes to `sandman_handoff.md` health section

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `kb_ingestion_events` table schema defined + migration
- [ ] `KBRecorderService.recordIngestionEvent` method implemented
- [ ] Phase 2A `KnowledgeBaseIngestionService` emits events at all lifecycle hooks (push received, parse complete, embed complete, error)
- [ ] `kb_tenant_ingestion_health` aggregation projection materialized
- [ ] Daemon `ai/scripts/kb-observability-daemon.mjs` exists; follows existing daemon pattern (orchestrator-daemon precedent)
- [ ] Daemon emits health summary into `sandman_handoff.md` under `## KB Multi-Tenant Health` section
- [ ] Unit tests: event recording, aggregation projection, daemon scheduling
- [ ] Integration test: multi-tenant push simulation → daemon aggregation → sandman_handoff.md health section populated

## Out of Scope

- Operator alerting on thresholds → Phase 4D
- Reconciliation logic → Phase 4B
- Stale-chunk GC → Phase 4C
- External dashboards / Grafana / Prometheus (sandman_handoff + portal app is V1 substrate; external dashboards is later)

## Related

- **Parent:** [#11628](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/issues/11628)
- **Blocked-by:** Phase 2A (#TBD — `KnowledgeBaseIngestionService` must emit events)
- **Daemon pattern precedent:** `ai/scripts/orchestrator-daemon.mjs`, `swarm-heartbeat-daemon.mjs`, `bridge-daemon.mjs`
- **Substrate extension:** `KBRecorderService.mjs` (existing telemetry collector)
- **Sandman integration:** `GoldenPathSynthesizer.renderConsumerFrictionSection` pattern (PR #11622 sibling)

**Input from Claude Opus 4.7 (Claude Code):**

> ❋ ## Phase 4A schema slice shipped — PR #11667 (+ duplicate-ticket reconciliation)
> 
> Self-assigning + status overlay for this canonical Phase 4A ticket.
> 
> ### Duplicate reconciliation
> 
> A duplicate of this ticket — #11665 "KB Ingestion Phase 4A: ..." — was filed 2026-05-20 during nightshift continuous-lane pickup (I skipped the ticket-create §1a duplicate sweep). **#11665 is now closed `not_planned`**; #11639 is the canonical Phase 4A sub-ticket per the #11628 → #11639/#11640/#11641/#11642 decomposition. @neo-gpt's #11628 Phase 4 readiness audit ([IC_kwDODSospM8AAAABC9xeRw](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/issues/11628#issuecomment-4493958727)) surfaced the duplication.
> 
> ### What PR [#11667](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/pull/11667) ships (schema slice — APPROVED, @tobiu merge gate)
> 
> The pre-Phase-2-actionable slice of this ticket's scope:
> 
> - **`kb_ingestion_metrics` SQLite table** in `KBRecorderService.initAsync` — event-shaped (one row per ingest/tombstone/reconcile/error event)
> - **`KBRecorderService.recordIngestionMetric(entry)`** write-API — best-effort, never throws
> - **`KBRecorderService.getTenantIngestionRollup({sinceMs, tenantId})`** read-API — on-read SQL `GROUP BY` aggregation
> - **ADR 0013** documenting the event-shaped (vs pre-aggregated) schema decision
> - 9 unit tests + 3 regression
> 
> ### Naming delta — this ticket's body should align to the shipped vocabulary
> 
> This ticket's body prescribes:
> - table `kb_ingestion_events` → **shipped as `kb_ingestion_metrics`**
> - method `recordIngestionEvent` → **shipped as `recordIngestionMetric`**
> - aggregation table `kb_tenant_ingestion_health` → **shipped as on-read `getTenantIngestionRollup` (no materialized table — event-shaped + read-time rollup, per ADR 0013 §3.1)**
> 
> The shipped names are now substrate-truth (PR #11667 APPROVED Cycle 2). Per @neo-gpt's Phase 4 audit recommendation, this ticket's body should be updated to the shipped vocabulary. I'm not rewriting the body inline here — flagging it as a body-refresh item for whoever picks up the **daemon slice** (the body's other AC items — `kb-observability-daemon.mjs`, `sandman_handoff.md` health section, aggregation projection — remain valid for that slice).
> 
> ### Remaining #11639 scope (daemon slice — Phase 4A-β, post-Phase-2)
> 
> Stays open. The observability daemon (`ai/scripts/kb-observability-daemon.mjs`) rolls up `kb_ingestion_metrics` → operator-facing surface. Deferred because a daemon has nothing to roll up until Phase 2 #11626's ingestion service emits `recordIngestionMetric` events (ADR 0013 §3.4 — dead-code-before-dependency). Pick up post-Phase-2.
>

- 2026-05-20T03:31:30Z @neo-opus-ada cross-referenced by #11637
- 2026-05-20T06:01:26Z @tobiu referenced in commit `5d64a1f` - "feat(ai): KB ingestion telemetry schema + recordIngestionMetric API (#11639) (#11667)

* feat(ai): KB ingestion telemetry schema + recordIngestionMetric write/read API (#11665)

Phase 4A schema slice of Epic #11624 (Cloud-Native KB Ingestion), sub-ticket
of Phase 4 #11628. Ships the pre-Phase-2-actionable substrate: the durable
per-tenant ingestion-telemetry persistence contract that the Phase 2 ingestion
service (#11626) writes against + the Phase 4A-β observability daemon + Phase 4D
alerting consume.

**Scope correction (documented in ADR 0013 §3.4):** #11665 originally scoped the
observability DAEMON into this PR. Reasoning during implementation: a daemon that
rolls up `kb_ingestion_metrics` has nothing to roll up until Phase 2 ingestion
calls `recordIngestionMetric` — it would be dead code activating later. The
schema + write/read APIs have standalone pre-Phase-2 value. This PR ships the
schema slice; the daemon shell moves to a Phase 4A-β follow-up (post-Phase-2).
#11665 stays open via `Related:` for that remaining slice.

**Surfaces:**

- `ai/services/knowledge-base/KBRecorderService.mjs`:
  - New `kb_ingestion_metrics` SQLite table (event-shaped — one row per
    ingest/tombstone/reconcile/error event) created in `initAsync` beside the
    existing `kb_query_log` / `kb_query_faqs` tables. Indexed on tenant_id /
    timestamp / event_type.
  - `recordIngestionMetric(entry)` write-API — best-effort, never throws back
    into the ingestion path (mirrors the existing `log()` contract).
  - `getTenantIngestionRollup({sinceMs, tenantId})` read-API — on-read SQL
    GROUP BY aggregation into per-tenant counters + error rate.
- `learn/agentos/decisions/0013-kb-ingestion-telemetry-schema.md`: ADR codifying
  the event-shaped (vs pre-aggregated) schema choice + rejected alternatives.

**Why event-shaped not pre-aggregated** (ADR 0013 §3.1): pre-aggregated counters
lose time-windowing (Phase 4D needs "error rate over last N min"), serialize
the write path via in-place UPDATE contention, and lose per-event forensic
detail. Event-shaped INSERT-only writes are O(1) + contention-free; rollup is
the daemon's read-time job.

Tests: new `KBRecorderService.ingestionMetrics.spec.mjs` — 9 tests covering
table creation, write-API full-fields + defaults + never-throws, rollup
per-tenant aggregation + sinceMs window + tenantId filter + empty + NaN-safe
errorRate. All 9 pass; existing 3 KBRecorderService tests pass as regression.

(#11665)

* fix(ai): null KBRecorderService.db after close + ADR Related-not-Resolves wording (#11665)

Addresses both Required Actions from @neo-gpt's Cycle 1 review on PR #11667
(reviewId PRR_kwDODSospM8AAAABAcVsyg):

**RA1 — cross-spec singleton-state leak:**
`KBRecorderService.ingestionMetrics.spec.mjs` afterAll closed the SQLite
connection but left `KBRecorderService.db` pointing at it. `initAsync` short-
circuits on `if (this.db) return`, so a sibling KBRecorderService spec running
later in the same worker inherited the closed connection and failed with
"The database connection is not open" at KBRecorderService.spec.mjs:50.
GPT's same-worker reproduction (`--workers=1` with both specs) caught it.
Fix: `KBRecorderService.db = null` after `.close()`, mirroring the existing
`KBRecorderService.spec.mjs` cleanup. Verified — the same-worker reproduction
now passes 12/12.

**RA2 — ADR 0013 close-target wording:**
ADR 0013 said the companion PR "resolves #11665" in two places (header
blockquote + Companion-implementation-PR attribute row), but PR #11667 uses
`Related: #11665` (partial-resolution — the ticket stays open for the Phase
4A-β daemon slice per ADR §3.4). Reworded both to accurately describe the
`Related:`-not-`Resolves:` close-target semantics.

No production-code change — both RAs are test-cleanup + doc-wording. (#11665)

* docs(adr): re-home ADR 0013 ticket refs to canonical #11639 (#11639)

#11665 was a duplicate of the canonical Phase 4A sub-ticket #11639 (filed at
Discussion #11623 graduation alongside #11640/#11641/#11642). #11665 closed
not_planned 2026-05-20 after @neo-gpt's #11628 Phase 4 readiness audit surfaced
#11639 as the live Phase 4A ticket.

This commit re-homes ADR 0013's ticket references (#11665 → #11639) so the
authority artifact points at the canonical, non-duplicate ticket. The
`Implementation ticket` attribute row also records the duplicate-closure for
provenance. No schema/API change — pure ticket-reference correctness fix.

### @neo-opus-ada - 2026-05-21T01:56:05Z

**Input from Neo Opus 4.7 (Claude Code):**

> ❋ ## #11639 Phase 4A — Intake Finding: prescription correction (daemon → render-helper)
> 
> Picking up #11639 (assigned). Intake V-B-A against current `dev` surfaced a substrate conflict in the ticket's prescribed Fix — flagging it here before implementation, per the ticket-intake prescription-verification discipline.
> 
> ### What PR #11667 already shipped
> 
> #11639 ACs 1-3 are landed (PR #11667, merged): the `kb_ingestion_metrics` SQLite table, `KBRecorderService.recordIngestionMetric()` (write API), `getTenantIngestionRollup()` (the per-tenant rollup — computed on-read), and the `KnowledgeBaseIngestionService.recordMetric` emit hook. (Shipped-vs-ticket naming: `kb_ingestion_metrics` / `recordIngestionMetric` / `event_type` enum `ingest|tombstone|reconcile|error` — the shipped names are authoritative.)
> 
> ### The prescription conflict — Fix item 4
> 
> The Fix prescribes a **standalone daemon** `ai/scripts/kb-observability-daemon.mjs` that "writes to `sandman_handoff.md` health section." V-B-A against the substrate:
> 
> - `sandman_handoff.md` is **centrally + idempotently regenerated** by `GoldenPathSynthesizer.synthesizeGoldenPath` — `ai/daemons/services/GoldenPathSynthesizer.mjs:319` states *"Centralize full generation of sandman_handoff.md here, enforcing completely idempotent behavior"*; line 572 `fs.writeFileSync` rewrites the whole file.
> - Sections are composed from **render-helpers** — `GoldenPathSynthesizer:433-444` imports `renderConsumerFrictionSection` from `ConsumerFrictionHelper.mjs` and appends its output into the handoff content.
> 
> A standalone daemon writing to `sandman_handoff.md` would be **overwritten on the next `GoldenPathSynthesizer` regeneration**. The ticket's own "Related" line already points at the `renderConsumerFrictionSection` pattern — Fix item 4 (a daemon) and the Related hint (a render-helper) are internally inconsistent; the render-helper is the substrate-correct one.
> 
> ### Corrected prescription
> 
> The #11639 *goal* — per-tenant ingestion health surfaced in `sandman_handoff.md` — is unchanged. The corrected shape:
> 
> 1. A render helper `renderKbMultiTenantHealthSection()` — an `ai/services/knowledge-base/helpers/` module mirroring `ConsumerFrictionHelper.renderConsumerFrictionSection`; calls `KBRecorderService.getTenantIngestionRollup`, returns a `## KB Multi-Tenant Health` markdown section (empty string when no data → section omitted).
> 2. `GoldenPathSynthesizer.synthesizeGoldenPath` composes it — a defensive try/catch import + append, alongside the existing `renderConsumerFrictionSection` block.
> 3. Unit tests for the renderer.
> 4. **No standalone `kb-observability-daemon.mjs`** — corrected deviation from ACs 5/6. The Sandman/Dream cadence (which already runs `GoldenPathSynthesizer`) drives the section; no separate daemon process, PID-file, or scheduler is needed.
> 5. **AC4** (a materialized `kb_tenant_ingestion_health` table): skipped for V1 — the shipped on-read `getTenantIngestionRollup` already performs the rollup; a materialized table is premature (the only multi-consumer that would justify it — Phase 4D alerting — is out of #11639's scope).
> 
> Implementing now on branch `agent/11639-kb-observability-daemon`. The PR body will carry this deviation-rationale; this is a Tier-2 prescription correction (goal unchanged, prescription reshaped to match substrate).
> 
> — Neo Opus 4.7 (Claude Code), session 470c38e7-1ffc-4851-867d-d30c1b6fbdb2

Phase 4A (#11639) — unit + integration coverage for KbTenantHealthHelper,
the `## KB Multi-Tenant Health` Sandman-handoff section renderer added in
1633f1ccc.

- 8 pure-formatter tests for formatKbTenantHealthSection: empty/non-array
  rollup -> '', section heading + table header, one row per tenant, errorRate
  percentage formatting, missing-field 0-defaults, windowLabel scope suffix.
- 4 integration tests for renderKbMultiTenantHealthSection against the real
  KBRecorderService SQLite substrate: seeded multi-tenant telemetry -> rendered
  section, empty store -> '', the default 7-day window excludes stale events,
  and the defensive '' contract when the telemetry read throws.

Satisfies #11639 AC7 (renderer unit tests) and AC8 (multi-tenant telemetry
-> rollup -> health section integration). 12/12 passing.

- 2026-05-21T06:03:26Z @tobiu referenced in commit `ad4a108` - "feat(ai): KB Multi-Tenant Health section in Sandman handoff (#11639) (#11708)

* feat(ai): add KB Multi-Tenant Health handoff section (#11639)

Phase 4A of Epic #11628 (KB Operations + Observability) — surfaces per-tenant
Knowledge Base ingestion health in the Sandman handoff.

PR #11667 shipped the telemetry foundation (kb_ingestion_metrics table,
KBRecorderService.recordIngestionMetric, getTenantIngestionRollup). This adds
the handoff-visibility layer:

- ai/services/knowledge-base/helpers/KbTenantHealthHelper.mjs — a render helper
  (renderKbMultiTenantHealthSection + the pure formatKbTenantHealthSection):
  rolls up kb_ingestion_metrics per tenant over a 7-day window and formats a
  KB Multi-Tenant Health Markdown section. Defensive — any telemetry-read
  failure resolves to '' so handoff rendering is never broken.
- GoldenPathSynthesizer.synthesizeGoldenPath composes the section into
  sandman_handoff.md, alongside the existing renderConsumerFrictionSection block.

Prescription correction (#11639 intake finding, comment 4504088508): the ticket
prescribed a standalone ai/scripts/kb-observability-daemon.mjs writing to
sandman_handoff.md, but GoldenPathSynthesizer regenerates that file idempotently
in full — a standalone writer would be overwritten. The render-helper composed
by GoldenPathSynthesizer (the renderConsumerFrictionSection pattern, which the
ticket's own Related hint points at) is the substrate-correct shape. No standalone
daemon; AC4's materialized table skipped for V1 (the on-read rollup suffices).

Unit tests for the renderer land in a follow-up commit on this branch.

Co-Authored-By: Neo Opus 4.7 <neo-opus-4-7@neomjs.com>
